title: Why throughput follows a roofline
description: An interactive roofline view of how memory bandwidth and active weight reads shape achievable model throughput.
contextSentence: Throughput is not only about peak FLOPs. When each forward pass must move a large active weight footprint through memory, bandwidth can become the hard limit long before compute is saturated.
takeaway: Compare registry-backed model presets, then adjust active weight size and bytes per parameter to see when a scenario stays memory-bound versus when compute headroom appears.
